items.title: 'How are you, mate?': Bodycam footage shown of moment police officer was gunned down

children.content: A court hears that some of the last words uttered by a policeman was to ask the man who fatally shot him, "how are you, mate?"

View of a funeral procession, with police officers lining a street as a hearse passes by.

items.title: Rex Airlines announces service cuts in Victoria and Tasmania

children.content: The embattled carrier has announced it is ceasing some services and reducing others.

image.altText: a plane flies in the sky

items.title: China and Russia block conservation bid for endangered emperor penguin

children.content: The annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations has failed to reach consensus to designate the endangered emperor penguin as a "Specially Protected Species".

image.altText: Several adult penguins and over a dozen chicks stand in a snowy landscape.

items.title: Sweeping cuts but little detail — can the government stick to the Abetz budget?

children.content: Since Eric Abetz became Tasmania's treasurer nine months ago, there have been two prevailing questions. How much will be cut? And what will be cut? As of yesterday, we know how much, but don't know what or where.

articles.title: What's being cut in Tasmania's state budget?

children.content: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

image.altText: Tas budget cuts cards graphic showing six categories.

articles.title: 'Hard to achieve, in our opinion': Ratings agency unimpressed with Tas budget

children.content: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.

image.altText: A photo taken over the shoulder of a male holding the blue Tasmanian Budget book.

gallery.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

thumbnail.altText: Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz and Premier Jeremy Rockliff in the budget lock-up for the 2026-27 state budget.

items.title: Dismay over 150-year-old house being reduced to rubble

children.content: How did a colonial-era mansion once earmarked to be on the state heritage register get bulldozed?

image.altText: An old black and white photograph of a house at 180 George Street, Launceston.

items.title: TassieCast: Who wins in Hawthorn's big breakup with Tassie?

children.content: Are you one of Tassie's 8,000-strong Hawks members? If so, you might be feeling a bit gutted right now as a messy divorce carries out after a 25-year partnership. Airlie Ward chats with Chris Rowbottom.

image.altText: Thousands of Hawks supporters greet team members in Launceston's Sliverdome

items.title: Stephen Colbert wraps up The Late Show with Paul McCartney and a fake pope

children.content: While Colbert's CBS network came in for plenty of jibes over the course of the episode, it was a largely apolitical affair, with attacks on US President Donald Trump ditched in favour of joy, jokes and songs.

gallery.altText: Five men in suits act out a superhero scenario backstage.

items.title: Appeal after request to keep challengers to corruption report secret denied

children.content: The release of a corruption report into Victorian government has been blocked, but the public has been denied the right to know by whom.

image.altText: A photo showing Ibac Independent Broad-Based Anti-Corruption Commission Victoria on the windows of its Victorian office.

items.title: 'Pink lady' denies drafting her own job description before council interview

children.content: The commission was shown a text to the former chief executive of Parramatta Council from Roxanne Thornton featuring a handwritten note detailing her salary and entitlements.

items.title: Farm worker found not guilty of circular saw amputation murder

children.content: The jury has returned its verdict in the case of a man who killed pensioner Kalman Tal. The 66-year-old died shortly after paying $5,000 to have his leg amputated in a park.

image.altText: John Yalu banana farm

items.title: Guzman Y Gomez soars on US exit, ASX climbs higher — as it happened

children.content: Australian markets have risen this morning, with Guzman Y Gomez surging on news it has pulled out of the US market.

items.title: The hidden codes and Aussie influence in Harry Connick Jr's new symphony

children.content: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

children.content: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a
bag of lettuce.

items.title: Diphtheria outbreak could be 'contained within months'

children.content: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.

image.altText: A woman holding up her t-shirt sleeve, revealing a small band-aid over the spot where she was vaccinated.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up skies across NSW

children.content: Colourful streaks across the skies have been spotted from Bondi to Canberra as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere overnight.

items.title: Three men charged after police discover alleged bomb-making lab

children.content: Three men have been charged with explosives and firearms offences as specialist police teams search a home in a quiet regional street.

items.title: What we know about Iran's new system of transit in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

image.altText: A drone view shows the Malta-flagged tanker Agios Fanourios I arriving in Iraq's territorial waters off Basra.

items.title: Cuba on the brink: Residents describe life 'trapped between two psychopaths'

children.content: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

articles.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says

children.content: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

image.altText: Marco Rubio speaking to reporters outside.

items.title: Inside the industrial-scale slave system run by Islamic State

children.content: Australia is the latest country to prosecute its own citizens over Islamic State's system of Yazidi slavery, part of a growing global reckoning over the role some foreign women allegedly played in the atrocities.

items.title: US prison officials call off prisoner's execution after 'botched' attempt

children.content: Corrections officials in Tennessee halt an inmate's execution by lethal injection after medical staff repeatedly fail to find a vein.

items.title: There are 40,000 trusts linked to Australian farms. Here's why

children.content: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

items.title: Snowy bosses paid $1.2m in bonuses as hydro project costs spiral

children.content: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

items.title: Global mental health disorder cases double in three decades

children.content: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

items.title: Aerial shooting to resume as brumby numbers spike in national park

children.content: Wild horse numbers in the NSW Snowy Mountains are back up with a reproductive control trial now being considered as aerial shooting has been confirmed to resume.

image.altText: A brown brumby with a blond mane stands in the sun in Kosciuszko National Park.

items.title: Chalmers asked to explain how he 'landed on 30 per cent' tax rate

children.content: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers speaks to reporters.

items.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor defends budget

children.content: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.
